name: Binche carnival
description: A family-friendly festivity featuring parades of harlequins, Pierrots, and peasants, dominated by Gille characters who dance to welcome spring.

name: Aalst carnival
description: A unique annual celebration where the town prince is awarded the keys to the city, featuring broom dancers and the burning of giant effigies.

name: Blancs-Moussis carnival
description: A historical celebration where participants dress as monks in white, throw confetti, and swing dried herrings to commemorate defiance against a 1502 ban on monks celebrating.

name: Knokke-Heist carnival
description: A festive carnival procession filled with music, dance, colourful outfits, and open-air concerts.

name: Ghent light festival
description: A triennial festival where international artists display artistic light structures and Ghent's monuments are illuminated.

name: Dour festival
description: A summer music festival featuring a wide range of genres including alternative, dance, pop, rock, electronic, punk, hip-hop, and techno.

name: Brussels Grand Place flower carpet
description: A biennial event where over 600,000 flowers form a massive, intricate carpet in the Grand Place, accompanied by a sound and light show.

name: Mons chocolate festival
description: A festival where local chocolatiers showcase and compete to offer the best Belgian chocolate treats.

name: Bruges beer festival
description: A celebration of Belgium's brewing reputation where visitors can sample hundreds of Trappist, craft, and speciality beers.
